purpose: DebugTool-facing evidence API derived from OpenDatasheet sch-review exports.

common_review_profile.review_philosophy: Treat datasheet/package facts as documented priors. Project schematics, measured waveforms, Quartus logs, IP parameters, and register counters are stronger target-system evidence.

known_global_gaps.impact: OpenDatasheet can expose package capabilities and pin facts, but DebugTool still needs project-specific net names, IP instance parameters, and measured evidence.
The interface links to the official Pin Connection Guidelines but does not yet encode every unused-pin and pull-policy table as machine rules.

package_pinout.source_revision_note:
Updated soft CDR support column:
-Added CDR support on pin index 0, 1, 12, 13, 24, 25, 36, and 37.
-Removed CDR support on pin index 10, 11, 22, 23, 34, 35, 46, and 47.

device_datasheet.scope: electrical, I/O, power, and configuration constraints

pin_connection_guidelines.source_note: The official Agilex 5 pinout workbooks reference the Pin Connection Guidelines for pin definition and connection guidance; this repository has not yet parsed that guide into structured rules.
pin_connection_guidelines.scope: manual schematic checklist until structured parsing is added

facts.claim: Configuration JTAG TCK is on package ball BJ58.
Configuration JTAG TDI is on package ball BJ53.
Configuration JTAG TDO is on package ball BN55.
Configuration JTAG TMS is on package ball BJ56.
Configuration status/control signal nCONFIG is on package ball BU32.
Configuration status/control signal nSTATUS is on package ball BV32.
Device exposes 138060 logic elements in the parsed capability profile.

required_evidence.tool_hint: jtagconfig -n and Quartus Programmer Auto Detect output
required_evidence.why: Separates host/cable enumeration, chain scan, device ID recognition, and soft debug nodes.

required_evidence.acquire_at: FPGA pins or closest accessible probes during Quartus Auto Detect

required_evidence.why: Target VREF, SDM/config bank power, and core rails gate JTAG visibility before bitstream-level hypotheses.
Mode straps, nCONFIG/nSTATUS, and configuration clock/status evidence should be acquired before changing programming files.

schematic_review_checks: Expose a measurable JTAG path at the FPGA end, not only at the connector.
Review TDI/TDO chain order, bypass devices, connector VREF, cable ground, and pull policy.
Check nCONFIG/nSTATUS, MSEL pins, OSC_CLK_1, AS/AVST boot pins, and SDM power rails before chasing Quartus software state.

known_gaps.impact: DebugTool's generic CONF_DONE/INIT_DONE checks must be translated to Agilex 5 package-specific configuration status evidence, primarily nCONFIG, nSTATUS, SDM_IO boot pins, and Quartus Programmer status.

required_evidence.why: MSEL and SDM_IO assignments select the boot path and should match the intended AS/AVST/JTAG configuration flow.
If JTAG can see the device but configuration fails, bus activity at the FPGA end separates flash/IP wiring from bitstream causes.
PWRMGT pins affect SmartVID and regulator interaction during configuration and early boot.

schematic_review_checks: Freeze the intended configuration mode and verify every SDM_IO multiplexed function against that mode.
Review AS_nCSO, AS_CLK, AS_DATA, AVST data/ready/valid, and AS_nRST routing at the package pins.
Check pull-ups, pull-downs, and unused SDM_IO handling against the Pin Connection Guidelines.

required_evidence.why: Agilex 5 bring-up faults can masquerade as JTAG/configuration failures when rails are non-monotonic or early rails brown out.
SmartVID/PMBus regulator behavior is part of the configuration-time evidence set, not a later optimization detail.

schematic_review_checks: Review monotonic ramp, regulator enable dependencies, sense routing, rail grouping, and decoupling before firmware-level hypotheses.
Keep rail names as net aliases so DebugTool can match measured waveforms to datasheet rails.

required_evidence.tool_hint: Quartus Programmer SOF success plus jtagconfig -n node list

schematic_review_checks: Do not treat ELF/debug failure after SOF success as a pure software problem until JTAG nodes, reset, clock, memory map, and exported hardware image match.

lane_group_mappings.selection_note: Freeze the protocol/IP assignment for this lane group and bind it to one of the mapped refclk pairs before schematic sign-off.

required_evidence.why: Transceiver rail noise and sequencing should be checked before protocol training or equalization changes.
PCIe/Ethernet/custom SerDes failures need refclk presence, frequency, standard, jitter budget, and pair-to-lane mapping evidence.

required_evidence.tool_hint: Transceiver Toolkit, PCIe HIP status, link training status, or protocol IP counters

schematic_review_checks: Freeze protocol, lane width, refclk frequency, and pair-to-quad mapping before schematic sign-off.
Check whether the selected ordering code and package support the intended transceiver use case.

required_evidence.why: DebugTool's MIPI path needs project binding facts because the package export does not know the chosen IP instance assignment.

schematic_review_checks: Treat MIPI as project-bound: lane assignment, polarity, termination, LP/HS state, and receiver counters must come from Quartus/IP/register evidence.
Use the package capability count only as a capacity prior, not as proof that a specific lane set is correctly assigned.

known_gaps.impact: The current Agilex 5 export exposes family/package MIPI capacity but not concrete D-PHY lane-to-ball assignments for a project IP instance.

required_evidence.tool_hint: Quartus EMIF Toolkit or calibration report
required_evidence.why: EMIF failures should be separated into calibration stage, lane/byte group, clock/reset, and board signal-integrity causes.
DQS/DQ grouping and bank selection are reviewable before board bring-up.

schematic_review_checks: Tie the chosen memory topology to banks, DQS groups, voltage rails, reference voltages, reset, clocks, and calibration observability.

schematic_review_checks: Freeze protocol, lane width, refclk frequency, and pair-to-quad mapping before schematic sign-off.
Check whether the selected ordering code and package support the intended transceiver use case.

required_evidence.why: DebugTool's MIPI path needs project binding facts because the package export does not know the chosen IP instance assignment.

schematic_review_checks: Treat MIPI as project-bound: lane assignment, polarity, termination, LP/HS state, and receiver counters must come from Quartus/IP/register evidence.
Use the package capability count only as a capacity prior, not as proof that a specific lane set is correctly assigned.

known_gaps.impact: The current Agilex 5 export exposes family/package MIPI capacity but not concrete D-PHY lane-to-ball assignments for a project IP instance.

required_evidence.tool_hint: Quartus EMIF Toolkit or calibration report
required_evidence.why: EMIF failures should be separated into calibration stage, lane/byte group, clock/reset, and board signal-integrity causes.
DQS/DQ grouping and bank selection are reviewable before board bring-up.

schematic_review_checks: Tie the chosen memory topology to banks, DQS groups, voltage rails, reference voltages, reset, clocks, and calibration observability.

required_evidence.why: HPS boot/debug failures need HPS rail and reset evidence before software image hypotheses.
Boot media, UART console, JTAG, SDMMC, EMAC, USB, and trace pins are muxed and must match the selected HPS design.

schematic_review_checks: DebugTool currently lacks a dedicated HPS boot link model; consume these facts as project-specific evidence inputs.

known_gaps.impact: A future DebugTool asset should model HPS boot straps, reset, clock, console, boot media, and secure boot/error-state evidence.

pin_connection_guidelines.source_note: The official Agilex 5 pinout workbooks reference the Pin Connection Guidelines for pin definition and connection guidance; this repository has not yet parsed that guide into structured rules.

facts.claim: Configuration JTAG TCK is on package ball CA109.
Configuration JTAG TDI is on package ball BW112.
Configuration JTAG TDO is on package ball BW109.
Configuration JTAG TMS is on package ball CA112.
Configuration status/control signal nCONFIG is on package ball BU99.
Configuration status/control signal nSTATUS is on package ball BW99.
Device exposes 138060 logic elements in the parsed capability profile.

required_evidence.tool_hint: jtagconfig -n and Quartus Programmer Auto Detect output
required_evidence.why: Separates host/cable enumeration, chain scan, device ID recognition, and soft debug nodes.

required_evidence.acquire_at: FPGA pins or closest accessible probes during Quartus Auto Detect

required_evidence.why: Target VREF, SDM/config bank power, and core rails gate JTAG visibility before bitstream-level hypotheses.
Mode straps, nCONFIG/nSTATUS, and configuration clock/status evidence should be acquired before changing programming files.

schematic_review_checks: Expose a measurable JTAG path at the FPGA end, not only at the connector.
Review TDI/TDO chain order, bypass devices, connector VREF, cable ground, and pull policy.
Check nCONFIG/nSTATUS, MSEL pins, OSC_CLK_1, AS/AVST boot pins, and SDM power rails before chasing Quartus software state.

known_gaps.impact: DebugTool's generic CONF_DONE/INIT_DONE checks must be translated to Agilex 5 package-specific configuration status evidence, primarily nCONFIG, nSTATUS, SDM_IO boot pins, and Quartus Programmer status.

required_evidence.why: MSEL and SDM_IO assignments select the boot path and should match the intended AS/AVST/JTAG configuration flow.
If JTAG can see the device but configuration fails, bus activity at the FPGA end separates flash/IP wiring from bitstream causes.
PWRMGT pins affect SmartVID and regulator interaction during configuration and early boot.

schematic_review_checks: Freeze the intended configuration mode and verify every SDM_IO multiplexed function against that mode.
Review AS_nCSO, AS_CLK, AS_DATA, AVST data/ready/valid, and AS_nRST routing at the package pins.
Check pull-ups, pull-downs, and unused SDM_IO handling against the Pin Connection Guidelines.

required_evidence.why: Agilex 5 bring-up faults can masquerade as JTAG/configuration failures when rails are non-monotonic or early rails brown out.
SmartVID/PMBus regulator behavior is part of the configuration-time evidence set, not a later optimization detail.

schematic_review_checks: Review monotonic ramp, regulator enable dependencies, sense routing, rail grouping, and decoupling before firmware-level hypotheses.
Keep rail names as net aliases so DebugTool can match measured waveforms to datasheet rails.

required_evidence.tool_hint: Quartus Programmer SOF success plus jtagconfig -n node list

schematic_review_checks: Do not treat ELF/debug failure after SOF success as a pure software problem until JTAG nodes, reset, clock, memory map, and exported hardware image match.
